txt: 'Eclectic' is for pop music with 'indigenous' percussion. *You're* a sartorial maverick.
'Gone rogue'? No, Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
'I feel it in my fingers... I feel it in my toes...'
'In the centre of this town there's a ghostly motorway', she sang.

'Subtle' like the aesthetic cacophony the two of you are making right now? Look at this shit. The visual fields make no sense. You're like a pair of white socks in brown sandals right now. It's disgusting.
*Are* there? Some of the other things are pretty bad.
*FALN "Ultra" -- wear the future!* You remember the slogan from some magazine...
*Idiot Doom Spiral*, huh? This is bound to be a good, high-concept conversation. At last!
*Ka-ching*!
*Lame...* That rings some bells... Could this be connected to Night City -- a.k.a. the *City of Rage*?
*Officer* could be an artistic statement. A claim to *official* renown.
*Officer* could be an artistic statement. You're already prone to those.
*This* is beyond primitive.
*This* limbed and headed machine of pain and undignified suffering is grinding to a halt. Tired of walking the desert, it doesn't want to feel or think anymore.
...an idea. Told to him by grown ups, from radio towers and leaflets, in beautiful print when he was still a teenager: everything is possible. If we fight.
...and to Humanity at large.
...like whorls of floorboards. The design looks organic, influenced by highly resistant wood materials, like Lignum Vitae and ebony perhaps.
...to no avail!
A *ghost insect*, he said? These people are looking for a ghost.
A *title* one might say even. One that draws inspiration from snoop fiction and Vespertine cop show staples.
A bit of experimenting every now and then isn't bad.
A book with ragged edges catches your notice. The front cover features a large, muscular man. The title reads: 'Man from Hjelmdall in the Lost City of the Pygmies'.
A brush -- an artist? The red splatter is... urban expressionism?
A chill runs down your spine as you envision a half-dozen people in professional attire standing around a chair awkwardly pretending to be waiting for a motor-bus. It's neither funny nor creative.
A conductor for the hundreds of story threads that pass through the Game Master's Frequency.
A downy blanket of white to cover up the miserable poverty of the scene.

A man like you can figure out his sexuality in a working day. It won't be twenty hours unless you want to enter the heightened realms of the *fantasme érotique* afterwards. Then it may be twenty hours *or more*! But that would be on your own time.
A name like armour.
A nickname -- who gave you this nickname, Klaasje?
A note of awe in her voice. She's shocked and pleased by her own audacity...
A paradigm-shattering revolutionary!?
A performance that would make the star of a radio melodrama blush.
A poet could write a dozen verses and still not begin to capture the profound vexation in that sigh.
A pretty *lame* oranjese name. Nowhere near *Cuno*... The thought crosses your mind and then it's gone. Perhaps something for later?
A quaint little box of radio waves.
A rhyme-smith? This is quite credible -- it goes with his cadence and way of speaking.
A silver jumpsuit falls off her like scale armour, sparkling. This is the sparkle of too many nights out on the city.
A simple little cadence. He seems to be making it up as he goes.
A strange coldness comes over you, as you look at the world. The waves sway the sloop, slowly. That's all.
A strong aura of *originality* surrounds the girl in question.
A tender type of hope. Something stirs in you... perhaps this is why *you* broke it?
A true man of ideas, equal to any of the great Dolorian polymaths.
A two-hearted spider.
Absolute destruction.
Absolutely. Now you just need the materials.
Actually there are a few -- cryptoanalyst, radio officer...
Aeons ago. Probably when you were still a child.
After all this time with Morell, he must have an opinion on cryptids -- this could lead to a good one.

Aggressive. Monotonous... but also somehow sacral. Primitive, yet futuristic, like a machine man. Nothing exists, but the here and now. All are one, one purpose... All you've managed is a list. The parts don't form a whole.
Ah, I see... A pair of waterblue shades. The writing on the left temple says: "Sub-Insulindic Rendezvous." The frame appears to be hand-carved out of bone.
Ah, so he *has* read something besides his books of abstruse theory!
Ahem. As a noted art cop you definitely have an opinion on this.
All of this sounds like something Ruby would be interested in...
All right. Let's consider the context and meaning here.
All that untaxed income must have fuelled *The New*. That can only mean one thing...
All the other walls on all the other houses must make a pilgrimage in adoration of this, the uncontested pinnacle of wall-craft. Colour peeled from the very face of god.
Alright, brainstorming time. Remember, this is just the ideation phase. There are no *bad* ideas.
Always the cynic. Then I have a suggestion -- just don't vomit any more, okay?
An *experimental* side-case.
An underground kingdom of shimmering spillways! Silver stars melted down and filtered for their precious discharge. What a place of inspiration!
And a bit of conceptual unity too, it being yellow and all...
And he's gone again. Looks like it's becoming a theme for him.
And now we both walk away and everybody lives.
And then there will be a giant statue of him, towering above it all?
And your mind's pretty clear, all things considered. In fact, it's too clear for you to be buying into this climbing vision quest.
Another day -- hustlin' and grindin' in the free world!
Are there actually two of them? Are you sure you're not just hallucinating or seeing double?
Are you kidding me? Fuel oil is so much cooler. No way you're disfiguring that beautiful wall with something as pedestrian as ordinary *paint*!

As if someone left out most of the night sky, filtering it through personal choice. The principle of this filter remains unknown to you. The thought dissipates and you feel as though you were only half-right.
As though the whole plaza were a giant radiocomputer, and the hostel its glowing filament memory.
As though you weren't envious enough of the boy as-is.
As you open your eyes, you should scream "HYPER! HYPER!" (It seems appropriate.)
Ask for his conclusion.
Ask what kind of races are there first. Classification is core to this stuff.
At least you can *think* about opening it. About doors in general -- they are, after all, fundamental to your life. Perhaps something useful will come from this?
Because -- you see it, finally -- this wall is *sublime*! LOOK AT IT! The shadows, the colours...
Because it's the most working class death you've ever seen.
Because this is no ordinary wall. It is *sublime*! LOOK AT IT! The shadows, the colours...
Below the confusion and rage -- a fit of jamais vu, like yours? The thought passes, more pressing matters take its place...
Bet their father's named Atticus Hardie -- Lucretia Hardie would be their sister. Anyway.
Beyond the unkempt backyard rises a monstrous assemblage of concrete, plaster, and corrugated sheet metal...
Big-up fucky-fingers? He *is* a *free-thinker*. Beyond the box. Can't argue with that.
Books in a *board game* section? Who wants to read books!?
Bravo! Continue, then, in the mediocre and vulgar epigones of the world. So what if everything is incomprehensibly shit and you can see it. Take no responsibility.
But all kinds of things could be wrong with you at this point -- you've known that much all along. Meanwhile, you still have a killer to catch.
But it's *not* lame! There's a real *tension* there... "City of Rage"...
But this isn't an *old school* case.
But this suffering -- it must have *some* kind of meaning... A story that will come out of it. Perhaps even a story that you will write yourself?
But, can't deny it -- your mind's pretty clear, all things considered. In fact, it's too clear for you to be buying into this climbing vision quest.
Called Volta do Mar, or Return from the Sea.
Can't bring the dead back.
Case closed, and with no time to spare. Ideologies are dangerous, but communism won't be troubling the people anymore -- all thanks to you, politico-detective.
Challenge accepted. You should be on the lookout for stylistic elements that elevate this cabbage to heights unforeseeable.
Clean white light, coming from the windows of a clean cube-shaped office building hidden amidst ruins. A secret...
Close your eyes. Reflect upon your core essence -- *really* bore into the depths of your soul and reach a hand toward the book stand.
Close your eyes. Unfold your mental map of Martinaise. It's morning. You're standing on the plaza before the Whirling-in-Rags.
Come on! Kings and sabres are so *played out*. There's a *better* way...
Come on, let's *really* impress them. Overturn the *race-class paradigm*. The more esoteric, the better.
Come on. Tell him what he wants to hear.
Communist theorists love puns, in case that wasn't obvious.

Concentration makes you squint your eyes. Your name should be deep gold and orange, like a forest fire looming on the horizon, but mixed with the stench of liquor rising from your breath. You're two steps closer to it, but there are still many to go...
Congratulations, you've somehow managed to fail at failing, which means, in a strange way, that you've succeeded?
Conjuring terrible visions of the future, which you apparently don't care for.
Could it be? Do their minds work in the same way?
Could it be? Maybe for him -- you only have a chapter or two left in you. Last of the penultimate, more like...
Could it mean *Klaasje* is an allacronym? For Katarzine Alasije?
Cultural victory? What is this then?
Dammit lieutenant, have you no intellectual curiosity?
Damn, it was too strong.
Dedicated. Hyperactive. Unified. You will have to add something of your own to understand this list of loosely formed qualities called hard core. You need your own entry. Make it!
Definitely not.
Deploying high-concept buzzword generator. All systems functional: Ready to engage in 3, 2, 1...
Do her words seem vague and abstract to you?
Do it. Do it for art.
Doesn't matter. The choice has been made. You caught the criminal -- It was Dick Mullen all along. Who's the real detective now? That's right, it's you. Rest easy tonight, *real detective*.
Doesn't sound like he particularly wants to be lumped in with them either. His is a scientific community of one.
Don't ask questions, roll with it. It will work, I promise!
Don't you see? Idiot Doom Spiral has finally achieved something *remarkable*.
Embrace your inner dweorg. You're a tall dweorg. Become the Dweorg King.
Endless are the wonders borne out of the cultural collaboration so wisely facilitated by the free world!
Even better, you're able to connect those themes to your critique of the novel's formal qualities, such as they are.

Exactly. It's not *only* your duty to only catch the criminals of the street, you must also apprehend criminals of the printing press and the gallery -- the trite and derivative artists and writers of the world!

Excellent question. Art is a diverse range of visual, literary, auditory, and performative creativity! It's an expression of imagination and technical skill. Additionally, it's history, criticism and pure enjoyment...
Explain himself? As if... explaining some kind of *work*? Some kind of avant-garde work way ahead of its time?
Extremely different from discussing the aesthetic merits of avant-garde literature while quaffing a fine Mirovan red, eh, Art Cop?
Face of a millipede trapped in amber...
Feld *Electrical*. How ironic -- all these dark rooms...
Find common ground with him, ease yourself in there.
Folded M dimension. A reference to the popular science fiction series *In System*. Look who's in a good mood suddenly (and reads science fiction).
For all the boys looking for adventure -- a blood spatter on the seas.
For him to stop reacting to stimuli, to be broken off from the world. Cordoned into darkness.
Forget about that! Something much more noteworthy goes off in your brain! Could it not have been that...
Forget this *public* art. She must have something private and esoteric. Something that tormented her until she brought it into existence.
From an imaginary fan.
Fucked? People after her? *Moralintern* people? This isn't Oranjese lit!
Go ahead and provide savage criticisms, Art Cop. The world is yours to rip to pieces and reassemble!
Go ahead, don't be shy. Just ask for race secrets.
Good pick.
Gradually, the pleasant sensation recedes. You turn your attention to the two young men, who appear just about finished.
Gravity anomaly? Diggin' it. Diggin' this para-scientific stuff right here.
Great gears are grinding to a halt. The machine is powering down. She's all out of jolt.
Gun? Yeah, you don't have that. Maybe you can *philosophize* your way out of it?
Hang on, you can do this. *Use your imagination*. You know who the suspects are, just... make it up.
Hanging? What a drag. He seems like a *cultured* gentleman -- you should ask him about the finer things...
Have you ever met anyone named 'Exilus'? Come on, they're plainly pseudonyms.

Have you looked in the mirror lately? You have the exact features of a savage art critic, with that beard and those clothes! Dishevelled and *prophetic*. Perhaps you should try to critique architecture too!
He does have potential... defying authority, thinking outside the box...
He doesn't even have a real name. What is inside this Man? Or is muscle and bone all he is?
He doesn't know your language, you can just say something cool in return.
He has a point. While an idea is born potentially immortal, flesh is always doomed to die.
He isn't even drawn right.
He looks almost innocentic, with that Harmon-Wowshi player raised up high. Could this be the certainty a spirit-of-the-world feels?
He said something about "saeraffic existence" before. Maybe this is what he was talking about?
He seems to be in some sort of *loop*.
He stares on, his wrinkled mouth moving without a sound -- a strange sadness, like a song.
He went with the 'Furies'. Perhaps the internal strife it implies was not so off after all? Or perhaps to honour your wishes? Or for some other reason. Hard to say...
He will *not* stop now, these dialectical materialist types never do. Exploit it.
He's interested in things that people believe that scientists don't.
He's probably one of those rare specimens who are born when two drunk seamen stumble on top of each other on the deck amidst a storm so violent it flings whales around.
He's right. His work would be much more formal.
He's right. This wasn't your grandma's game studio, this is something else.
Hello, Girard. Technically speaking, you're electricity.
Her neurons must be on fire, the heat creating waves and ripples in all she sees -- above all, in her painting.
Here we go. Two different approaches to cap this off with style.
Hermeneutics was almost within your grasp, but now only vague letters float before your eyes. Less meaningful, but aesthetically more pleasing.
Hey! You're not just 'some cop', you've got highly developed critical faculties! Now's your chance to show them off.
His eyes are your eyes.
His personality is no longer a part of the world.
His slender figure is backlit by city lights, its distant streets and motorways flashing like diamonds...
His smile is so wide it could blanket the universe.
His theory isn't exactly *incoherent*, but its logic does suggest some *unusual* neural activity. Interesting...
Historical revisionism has taken place.
History classes. Students with their textbooks open, studying the roots of our civilization. Those aquarelle-blue tiles looked beautiful in the sun.
Hmm... it *was* interesting. No arguing against that.
Hmm... maybe if you inquire more about the present, you'll figure out the secret of returning to the past.
Hmm... maybe that *would* work? We'll have to see.
Hmm... maybe you should ditch music as well, get into these more *experimental* sounds he's describing?
Hmm... perhaps you could compress this negative energy and turn it into some sort of a Cunofied Nonvomitor?
Hmm... the level of conceptualization *could* always be higher, yes.
Hmmm, this really didn't seem like it was going to be that hard. We'll just have to try again...
Home?
Hordes of wild homeless people roaming the lands, nomads scattered across an endless plain in a dystopian world where tare is the only valid currency... and people eat each other to stay alive.
How can they fit into all the holes and gaps? Are they very tiny people? Or are they... eyes, separated from the body?
Hyper-productive vegetables and ultra-horny communards are fine, but this theory hasn't quite gotten strange enough for you.
I am so disappointed in you. What *are* you interested in, then?
I bet she hasn't even heard it.
I don't know... have you seen how *basic* that thing is? Basic clip, unglamorous toilet-smelling papers. It could've done with some *pizzazzo*! Anyway, go on. Sorry I said anything.
I hate to interrupt the deafening pain you're in, but Joyce said it was possible to force dimensions by using radio waves. This must be one of the devices used to send radio signals across the pale.
I'm sorry. I'm foam. All I can do is foam, it's meaningless.
I'm sure we can come up with something with a bit more *oomph* than that.
I'm sure you did. You have an exploratory, creative streak -- just like them.

IF YOU FEAR IT, LET IT HAPPEN.
KEEP ON DANCING TILL THE WORLD ENDS.
Ice... death... loss... sounds like you.
If anything, it sounds a bit *proto*. Like it's not fully formed yet. You might be a moribund alcoholic and a failed cop, but you are pretty certain a thing cannot be both *proto* and *hard core*.
If business planning were really your strong suit, you probably wouldn't be a cop.
If one hornet can kill 40 honeybees in a minute, then three hornets should be able to kill 120 honeybees in a minute... Or more, since they'll also be covering one another.
If points are really *arbitrary*, as the lieutenant says, what's to stop you from playing the game the way *you* want?
If there ever was such a thing as an ugly kid, then this is it. He's almost exquisite in his ugliness. Like a gremlin.
If they put this much effort into making welkin cooler, imagine what they did with the dweorg and the humans?
If this transceiver were a person, it would be an accountant at a large logistics firm. Perfectly competent, but unexceptional.
If trees were made of porcelain, this is what their cross-sections would look like.
If you declare your life forfeit; if you declare yourself a doomed man... A doomed man does not need comfort. From *anything*. He can go on without drink, or help. Like a clockwork.
Imagine, the audacity of wanting to read a novel in a reading group!
Imbecilic. Yes, should the future ever come, it will look deeply imbecilic. Like this guy.
Immortalized.
In short, art is the highest form of human communication -- representation, narrative, emotion and agency intertwined.
In the background, a quiet song seeps from her cabin into the air. You don't hear any vocals.
In truth, these sound more like scenes from some old fantastique novel, the product of the author's fevered imagination rather than reality.
In what is called the IL -- the International Language, developed by scientists from Graad in the Twenties! Sinus means 'Sign', for example.
In your mind you could still climb inside her bed. Still the only man...
Indeed, what were the other ideas?
Instead of air, you exhale thoughts. There are no trees that eat thoughts.
Intellectually speaking... it would be quite *interesting* to hear what she has to say about these things...
Interesting term. Time to glean some knowledge!
Interesting. Are we speaking of visual art or something more *conceptual*?
Interesting. He wants you to *describe* it, though he already knows what it looks like.
Interesting. You can immediately see multiple ways to interpret this piece. What is the *experience* that the artist is trying to create? What *bodies* and *spaces* are they seeking to explore?
Investigation upon investigation, here in Martinaise, racing toward some dark deadline.
Is it not the wisest man who knows he knows nothing?
Is it? Yet your eye constantly seeks out the most critical side of any aesthetic confrontation. You follow the expressions of imagination and technical skill with curiosity...
Is this Oranjese lit?
Isn't it... evil? The order of magnitude between what is asked of a person -- and what they have?
Isn't that what *real* artists do? Make it up as they go along?
It *is* rather interesting to tell people things about each other isn't it? It was nice telling him about her right now...
It *must* be. This is the greatest and kindest arrangement the atoms had in them.
It almost looks as if the stones and dice are a natural part of the room, growing out of the shelves like stalagmites.
It also makes *Klaasje* almost an allacronym -- for Katarzine Alasije.
It can still be an otherwordly sex-mystery *in your head*. With a dark twist, even.
It feels like a *spell*.
It is -- look at how basic that thing is! Basic clip, unglamorous toilet-smelling papers. It could do with some *pizzazzo*" Cheer you right up.
It is.
It looks like the artist is celebrating diversity, but underneath he's just making fun of these people.
It reminds you of something, but you can't think of what. Oh well, let's see if the students are still going...

It says: everyone look at me and my *kitchen tissue* covered cop ledger! I don't *care*. My ledger is droopy and  smells like a urinal. Not a new addition, though. So keep that *styling case* open, my friend...
It says: everyone look at me and my *kitchen tissue* covered cop ledger! I don't *care*. My ledger is droopy and it smells like a urinal.

It says: everyone look at me and my toilet paper covered cop ledger! I don't *care*. My ledger is droopy and it smells like a urinal. Not a new addition, though. So keep that *styling case* open, my friend...
It says: everyone look at me and my toilet paper covered cop ledger! I don't *care*. My ledger is droopy and it smells like a urinal.
It was probably white and gold, with light red flower motives. Part of you -- assumed to be lost to nerve damage -- knows this style to be Ubi Dolorianism.
It was you. Coming from the west. From the Whirling. You were coming...
It would be an artistic statement of some kind or another.
It'll take more than rain to bring this place back to life.
It's *interesting* time. Forget about the rest.
It's a puzzle -- what's hanging in front of you is a puzzle of decaying flesh, tattoos, and tendons.
It's a secret rite. A very fringe-nationalist handshake, probably.
It's all part of the Masterplan, you see.
It's almost an anthropological sight: watching him try to assert dominance over you.
It's almost like music, especially with the sounds of assorted dishes boiling and simmering on the stove.
It's an empty vessel, an amphora waiting to be filled. Something always *returns* from the past -- it's how the future happens. And when it does, many guesses turn out to have been right.
It's death -- but for the Universe? Oh, we're contemplating the living *shit* out of this.
It's even worse than she says. God is dead -- We live in a forsaken age.
It's hardly a side-investigation. You already have a name for it.
It's like a little engine has come alive on the other side of the fence. An engine that only says *kipt*.
It's like an organic sample brought back from a distant star system inhabited by sentient neckwear.
It's like she's... disappointed this wasn't about more entertaining matters.
It's like some red infection was spreading outwards from the container yard's core.
It's no mere brain.
It's no mere turn of phrase. This young woman has a deeply rooted understanding of what *sad* means.
It's not a very good mother-joke, but the room still laughs. Something about those mother-jokes just *works*.
It's not exactly poetry -- but poetry would be out of place.
It's official. He too agrees. This is the antechamber of the afterlife.
It's on the tip of your tongue -- a *doubt* of sorts, already in your head -- but it's not fully formed yet. You have to wait and return to this later, once the *Jamais Vu* thought is complete.
It's symbolic of vice and sin.
It's the abyss staring back at you.

It's the contrast between the cheery, chemical yellow and the rigor mortis. As if the cheery guy didn't know he was dead or the dead guy didn't care that he was. Either way, you get the sense the forces of *future* are at work here.
It's the stilted language and overconfident tone that's the real giveaway here. You're almost certainly reading a recycled university essay, one that could probably use some edits.
It's the work of a self-taught visionary.
It's time to disrupt the future. You've got to stay lean, innovative, and focus on what matters most: *the children*.
Its insect mind is impenetrable to your reasoning.
JUMPING ALL OVER THE WORLD!
Jerry is a cool name.
Just a minute. Steban... Ulixes... *why* do those names ring the faintest of bells?
Let them know your interest in muscleman literature is much more high-minded than they're giving you credit for.
Life gets haaaaaaaaaard. But we go ooooooooooooooon...
Life-world? Someone's been reading up on last century Gottwaldian philosophers. Play it cool now.
Like a cancer of the blood, metastasising...
Like a cat rubbing itself against its owner's calves. (A cat that wants you to smoke a lot.)
Like a crossword puzzle solver. Or a master at the guessing game Peng.
Like a machine.
Like a silent orchestra tuning -- at the beginning of some major work.
Like a submarine fuelled by suffering.
Like a tiny homunculus, the dweorg lives within your blue soul.
Like love.
Like skyscrapers, spires of dirt and sand rising. Accommodations for their insectoid inhabitants?
Like that evil ink that filled the printout, erasing coherence and meaning.
Like the last rays of the evening sun gently kissing the day goodbye, before giving way to unfathomable darkness.
Like two baby crocodiles.
Literary demotion! That's the worst...
Look: The skis and rotor blades both bear the same "Slipstream" logo. It seems likely that they started out making one, failed to turn a profit, and then pivoted to producing the other.
Looks like someone tried to *reconceptualize* their business here.
Lord of the western plain! It is really happening, right here, the migrants are contaminating our youth!
Made of black film and folding tape structures...
Makes you feel *rebellious*.
Maybe it's a little old-fashioned? There's a nude mag? More than that, you can't say.
Maybe it's the second part of the leitmotiv you saw on the stained glass window.
Maybe it's true -- the hanged man is merely a prop in a performance, we are the audience, and the artist is hiding somewhere in the dark?
Maybe the church was designed in this way to prevent boisterous activity -- singing and dancing -- on its premises?
Maybe you could *paint* something with this coal? Leave a cave painting for future archaeologists... no, that would be stupid.
Metaphor. It's always metaphor.
More important than a missing *expedition?* I don't know... expeditions often lead to something interesting.
Most of these titles seem to be in the sci-fi genre. Some thrillers too.
My money is on cool. Looks like a cubic pyrite.
Nah, that's just the smell of healthy experimentation. And lots of chemicals.
Naked women and giant swords -- is that really the best they could come up with? Feels formulaic and... derivative.
No luck. All you find is this... lime-coloured cellophane visor? Produced by a bargain sportswear brand called Amphibian, apparently. There's a malformed green frog on its bent cap.
No one's telling you to like him. You might learn something from him, though.
No! Stop! He's already mortified.
No, it wouldn't. It would be thematic.
No, it's more like a creature from the abyssopelagic zone, a great dark bioluminescent fish...
No, it's not. Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau sounds solemn and historical. This is an opportunity to start your life anew as a *classier* person.
No, not presence -- *Absence*.
No, wait! Can this really be the end? You feel like you've just gotten to the real stuff.
No, you weren't. Otherwise you *would have* said it. In truth, it was quite beyond your associative powers.
No, you won't. Raphaël Ambrosius Costeau sounds solemn and historical. This is an opportunity to start your life anew as a *classier* person.
No, you're a proud warrior, keep it.
No-no. It's great.
No. Just no. I can already see it's failed and I'm *stopping* it now. Until you have *good* poetry -- you have *no* poetry.
No. You *must* know.
Nope, nothing. The idea factory is closed until further notice.
Nope, you're already telling it.
Not *really*. It's just stylization. The way they drew eyes -- it's not a zoological drawing.
Not just any face... it reminds you of the grotesque carvings sometimes found in prehistoric caves.
Not just any fish, it moves like some ancient bottom-feeder gliding over the ocean floor in search of prey...
Not quite. You *do* know that there's something unusual about this company's business model...
Not that you can tell. This is a digest of someone who's dead bored. Most of it is for entertainment purposes, fittingly right next to the *radiola* on the floor.
Nothing. Just time passing. Don't worry.
Now *that* is intriguing. You had me at Doorgunner.
Now maybe she'll open up about those *fascinating cryptids*. Take her mind off all this...
O WALLFATHER!
Of course -- no time for silly infracultures. There are more pressing, reality-centric questions to be asked.
Of course it tastes vile. Look at the effort you put in the culinary side of this? Not exactly fine dining, is it?

Of course not, it's autism. Box-drawing. Masturbation with a ruler and a sextant or whatever they use. You should demean and criticize the genteel institution of architecture. While extolling the virtues of the *pure* arts.
Of course you want to go deeper. What else are you here for?
Of course! They're the ones who produced that overwrought *critique* of TipTop Tournée.
Of course.
Of course... You slippery bastard.
Of course: there is a moral to be drawn from it -- a moral to this story!
Of what and to whom, you think? Spoken as the something that will one day return? Or as the someone who's waiting?
Oh no, it's one of those 'show, don't tell' things, isn't it?
Oh no. He's pretty much a bum now.
Oh yeah it feels manly. Beautiful.
